commit.message:
Run real vLLM instances in disaggregated serving demo

Launch two vLLM servers (Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct) as Prefill and Decode,
route requests through the proxy, and send test prompts end-to-end.
Demonstrates the full Mooncake proxy pattern with real LLM inference.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add vLLM disaggregated serving proxy for real Mooncake + vLLM deployment

Implements a simplified version of Mooncake's vllm_v1_proxy_server.py that
routes requests between vLLM Prefill (kv_producer) and Decode (kv_consumer)
instances. Supports round-robin load balancing across multiple nodes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: nano-mooncake — Mooncake minimal prototype in ~650 lines of Python

Implements the core design of Mooncake (FAST 2025 Best Paper) as a teaching prototype:
- Transfer Engine with pluggable TCP transport and batch async transfers
- Mooncake Store with Master/Client, two-phase put, replica tracking, and lease eviction
- HTTP metadata server for segment discovery
- Two runnable examples: Prefill/Decode disaggregation and distributed KVCache store

fix: sticky search bar and filter controls during scroll (#195)

* fix: make search bar and filter controls sticky during scroll

The library header controls (search, platform filter, sort, favorites,
view mode toggle, and results count) now stay pinned at the top of the
scroll container while browsing the game grid. Cards scroll smoothly
behind the opaque sticky header.

Also adds a stacking context (relative z-0) to the scroll container so
card z-indices don't escape above the Library header and system tabs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: pass explicit config to oxlint to fix lint in worktrees

Oxlint walks up the directory tree to discover config files. In git
worktrees (nested under the main repo), it discovers both the
worktree's and the main repo's oxlint.config.ts. Each loads
defineConfig() from a different node_modules instance, causing a
WeakSet validation mismatch that fails the config parse.

Fix: pass -c ../../oxlint.config.ts explicitly so oxlint uses only
the repo-root config without walking parent directories.

release: v0.1.9 (#808)

* release: v0.1.9

* fix: exclude docs-site from root TypeScript compilation

The root tsconfig.json includes `**/*.ts` which picks up files in
docs-site/, a separate Next.js project with its own path aliases.
This caused `next build` to fail resolving `@/lib/source` from the
docs-site project. Adding docs-site to the exclude array fixes CI.

Add CAT diagnostics panel with trace bus and debug snapshot export (v0.67.1)

Phase 1 of the radio configuration/diagnostics tool. Instruments the
entire CAT command pipeline (queue, transport, parser) with structured
trace events captured in a ring buffer. Diagnostics panel shows real-time
command trace, error counters, and connection summary. Export Debug Bundle
button downloads a JSON snapshot for AI-assisted troubleshooting.

New files:
- src/cat/diagnostics/trace-bus.js — ring buffer event collector
- src/cat/diagnostics/snapshot.js — JSON debug bundle generator
- src/cat/diagnostics/diag-panel.js — diagnostics UI panel

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fused push+reduce kernel: 39% faster allreduce

Two new optimizations for PCIe topologies without NVLink/switch:

1. Fused kernel: merges cudaMemcpyAsync + kernel launch into single
   kernel, saving ~5-8μs host API overhead per call.

2. Push model: each GPU writes (posted PCIe TLPs, fire-and-forget) to
   all peers' IPC buffers, then reduces from own buffer (local HBM).
   Replaces slow non-posted PCIe reads with fast posted writes.

Push mode activates when buf_capacity is passed to register_pcie_buffers
and msg_size * world_size fits in the buffer. Falls back to fused-pull
(still saves 1 API call) for larger messages.

Results (4x Blackwell RTX PRO 6000, same NUMA, bf16 14KB):
  Before: 27.4 μs/AR, 3.3 ms/tok
  After:  16.7 μs/AR, 2.0 ms/tok  (39% faster)

commit.message:
fix: group leaderboard weekly tab to filter by group.startDate

The weekly leaderboard was counting workouts from before the group was created
(if they fell within the current calendar week), while the total leaderboard
only counted workouts after group.startDate. This caused Total < Week for
members who logged workouts before the group started.

Now both the current-week and last-week queries use Math.max(monday, group.startDate)
as the lower bound, ensuring consistency: Total >= Week always.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: migrate from Thirdweb to Privy embedded wallet + gas station

Replace Thirdweb's enclave wallet (1.3-2.2s server round-trip per sign)
with Privy's MPC on-device signing (<5ms). Users now broadcast directly
to our RPC as standard EOAs — no relayer in the tx path.

Auth: Privy hooks (usePrivy, useWallets, useLoginWithOAuth) replace
Thirdweb's inAppWallet/viemAdapter/getUserEmail. Context interface
simplified — removed embeddedWallet, thirdwebClient, thirdwebChain.

MUDContext: Removed double-layer Proxy wrapper. Direct WalletClient
with writeObserver. Gas override proxy for UD__move (4M fixed gas).

Relayer → Gas Station: Deleted tw_execute/tw_getTransactionHash/
tw_getDelegationContract. Added POST /fund endpoint with anti-griefing
(rate limits, IP limits, double-fund check). New balanceMonitor polls
funded addresses every 60s, tops up when low.

Gold Merchant: Replaced Thirdweb BuyWidget + DOM manipulation hacks
with MoonPay iframe + Uniswap V3 ETH→Gold swap. Clean state machine.

useGasStation: Enabled for embedded users (was relayer-only).

Added /privacy and /terms pages for MoonPay API compliance.

absolute dates. Filters on updatedAt for list/stale and createdAt for
ready. Also updates help, prime, and agent instructions documentation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add per-key advisory locks to eliminate deadlocks

Lock ordering alone was insufficient - 3-way deadlock cycles could still
form between DELETE and INSERT operations at different transaction stages.
Advisory locks (pg_advisory_xact_lock) now serialize all writes to the
same key, preventing any deadlock possibility.

commit.message:
v3.3.2: Add FailPoint #8 — stale project instructions caused repeated failure

FailPoints:
- Added #8: Stale Project Instructions Caused Repeated Failure (2026-03-08)
  - Agent definitions were updated for Docker deployment (FailPoint #7)
    but the project's CLAUDE.md still referenced launchctl/local pyenv
  - Orchestrator followed the stale CLAUDE.md instructions, continuing to
    ask the user to run Docker commands manually
  - Same class of failure repeated within the same session
  - Principle: fixes must be complete — updating agent definitions without
    updating the project instructions the orchestrator actually reads is
    a partial fix that guarantees recurrence

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add order-independent equality for Triangulation

Tetrahedron gets sortedVertices() and opCmp so tets can be sorted
lexicographically by their vertex labels. Triangulation gets opEquals
that compares as unordered sets of tets, ignoring both tet list order
and vertex order within each tet.

The 4-1 round-trip test now uses opEquals directly to verify the
original triangulation is recovered.

commit.message:
fix: run eventsproc as non-root user, fix SQLite write permissions

Dockerfile: create dedicated eventsproc system user and run container
as non-root for improved security posture.

lab-setup.sh: chmod events.db to 666 after checkpoint table init so
the non-root eventsproc user can write checkpoints in SQLite mode.
NetBird creates events.db as root:root 644 — world-write is acceptable
in this single-host lab environment.
